name_en: Documents of the Miyagawa family
description_en: A collection of documents of the Miyagawa family. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 12, 1981. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of Chotokuji Temple
description_en: A collection of documents of Chotokuji Temple. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 13, 1990. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of Seitokuji Temple
description_en: A collection of documents of Seitokuji Temple. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 13, 1990. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 3-7-22, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of Shinkaiji Temple
description_en: A collection of documents of Shinkaiji Temple. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 10, 1992. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 2-9-5, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Adjudication document concerning the Kachi Shinshuku supplementary post station of Shinagawa
description_en: An adjudication document (saikyojo) concerning the Kachi Shinshuku supplementary post station of Shinagawa. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on April 10, 1997. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of Shinagawa Shrine
description_en: A collection of documents of Shinagawa Shrine. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 23, 1999. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 3-7-15, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of the Udagawa family
description_en: A collection of documents of the Udagawa family. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 23, 1999. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of the Oshima family
description_en: A collection of documents of the Oshima family. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 19, 2001. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of Gangyoji Temple, 3 scrolls, 18 items
description_en: A collection of documents of Gangyoji Temple, comprising 3 scrolls and 18 items. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on August 24, 2004. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 2-1-12, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Documents of Mitake Shrine
description_en: A collection of documents of Mitake Shrine. It is a tangible cultural property (calligraphy and classical books) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 28, 2013. It is kept at the Shinagawa Historical Museum, Oi 6-11-1, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Hōzenji Itabi Hōkyōintō Gorintō Tsuketari Ifunhi Banreitō Kokotsu no Fun Nihyakunanajūgoten
name_en: Stone stupa slabs, hokyointo pagodas, and five-ring pagodas of Hozenji Temple, with memorial monuments, an all-souls tower, and an ossuary mound, 275 items
description_en: Stone stupa slabs (itabi), hokyointo pagodas, and five-ring pagodas of Hozenji Temple, accompanied by memorial monuments, an all-souls tower, and an ossuary mound, totaling 275 items. It is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 12, 1983. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 2-2-14, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Official notice board of Soeura (1)
description_en: An official notice board (kosatsu) of Soeura. It is a tangible cultural property (historical material) designated by Shinagawa Ward. It is located at Higashi-Shinagawa 1-35-8, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Whale monument
description_en: A stone monument known as the whale monument. It is a tangible cultural property (historical material) designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 28, 2006. It is located at Higashi-Shinagawa 1-7-17, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Foundation stone of the five-story pagoda of Tenmyokokuji Temple
description_en: The foundation stone of the five-story pagoda of Tenmyokokuji Temple. It is a tangible cultural property (historical material) designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 25, 2009. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 2-8-23, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Fuji mound of Shinagawa Shrine
description_en: The Fuji mound (fujizuka), a miniature replica of Mount Fuji, at Shinagawa Shrine. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 3-7-15, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Stone memorial tower of the nenbutsu confraternity at Zenpukuji Temple, one tower
description_en: A stone memorial tower of the nenbutsu confraternity at Zenpukuji Temple, one tower. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 1-28-9, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Stone Koshin memorial tower of Hongakuji Temple, one tower
description_en: A stone Koshin memorial tower at Hongakuji Temple, one tower. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 11, 1980. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 1-10-11, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Color-on-paper painting of scenes of hell, one hanging scroll
description_en: A color-on-paper painting depicting scenes of hell, one hanging scroll. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 12, 1981. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 2-2-14, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Printing blocks of Kaitokuji Temple, 20 blocks
description_en: A set of 20 wooden printing blocks of Kaitokuji Temple. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 12, 1981. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 1-2-10, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Kaiunji Sentai Kōjindō Hōnō Hengaku Nijūnanamen
name_en: Votive plaques dedicated at the Sentai Kojin Hall of Kaiunji Temple, 27 plaques
description_en: A set of 27 votive plaques dedicated at the Sentai Kojin Hall of Kaiunji Temple. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 14, 1985. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 3-5-21, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Color-on-paper paintings of the six realms, five panels
description_en: Color-on-paper paintings of the six realms of existence (rokudo), five panels. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on July 25, 2002. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 2-8-16, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Printing blocks and copper plates of Hozenji Temple
description_en: Wooden printing blocks and copper printing plates of Hozenji Temple. It is a tangible folk cultural property designated by Shinagawa Ward on July 22, 2008. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 2-2-14, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Road marker inscribed with the date Kyoho 21
description_en: A road marker bearing an inscription of the year Kyoho 21. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 4-1-15, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Grave of Itagaki Taisuke
description_en: The grave of Itagaki Taisuke. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 3-7-15, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Burial grounds of the Okudaira family
description_en: The burial grounds of the Okudaira family. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 4-2-35, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Shibukawa Shunkai Haka (Tsuizō Kinentō o Fukumu)
name_en: Grave of Shibukawa Shunkai (including the posthumous honor memorial tower)
description_en: The grave of Shibukawa Shunkai, including the memorial tower commemorating his posthumous honor. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 4-11-8, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Site of the main inn of the Shinagawa post station on the Tokaido
description_en: The site of the main inn (honjin) of the Shinagawa post station on the Tokaido highway. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 2-7-21, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Site of the government-operated Shinagawa glass factory
description_en: The site of the government-operated Shinagawa glass factory. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 4-11-5, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Grave of Matsudaira Yoshinaga (Shungaku)
description_en: The grave of Matsudaira Yoshinaga, also known as Shungaku. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 5-16, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Grave of Yuri Kimimasa
description_en: The grave of Yuri Kimimasa. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 5-16, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Group of memorial towers for the unclaimed dead at Kaizoji Temple
description_en: A group of memorial towers for the unclaimed dead at Kaizoji Temple. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on November 22, 1978. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 4-4-2, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Ryūmin Sōzukahi Tsuketari Namu Amida Butsu Hi Banrei Nōkotsudōhi Ikki
name_en: Monument of the common burial mound for displaced people, with the Namu Amida Butsu monument and all-souls ossuary monument, one monument
description_en: A monument of the common burial mound for displaced people, accompanied by a Namu Amida Butsu monument and an all-souls ossuary monument (one monument). It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 14, 1986. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 2-2-14, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Grave of Tochuken Kumoemon
description_en: The grave of Tochuken Kumoemon. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on December 25, 2007. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 2-8-23, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Stone-built revetment of the Shinagawa post station on the Tokaido
description_en: A stone-built revetment of the Shinagawa post station on the Tokaido highway. It is a historic site designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 29, 2023. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 2-14-10, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Ginkgo tree of Honsenji Temple
description_en: A ginkgo tree at Honsenji Temple. It is a natural monument designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 14, 1978. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 3-5-17, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Ginkgo tree of Kashoku Inari Shrine
description_en: A ginkgo tree at Kashoku Inari Shrine. It is a natural monument designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 14, 1978. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 2-32-3, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Ginkgo tree of Hozenji Temple
description_en: A ginkgo tree at Hozenji Temple. It is a natural monument designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 14, 1978. It is located at Kita-Shinagawa 2-2-14, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Chinquapin tree of Dairyuji Temple
description_en: A chinquapin (shii) tree at Dairyuji Temple. It is a natural monument designated by Shinagawa Ward on February 14, 1978. It is located at Minami-Shinagawa 4-2-16, Shinagawa Ward.

name_en: Mountain-opening ceremony of the Fuji mound at Shinagawa Shrine
description_en: The mountain-opening ceremony held at the Fuji mound (fujizuka) of Shinagawa Shrine. It is a cultural property in the manners and customs category designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 14, 1986. It takes place at Shinagawa Shrine, Shinagawa Ward.

name_romaji: Shinagawa Byōshi (Shinagawa Jinja Chūshin no Chiiki)
name_en: Shinagawa rhythm music (area centered on Shinagawa Shrine)
description_en: Shinagawa byoshi, a traditional festival rhythm music transmitted in the area centered on Shinagawa Shrine. It is a cultural property in the manners and customs category designated by Shinagawa Ward on March 24, 1987. It is practiced in the area around Shinagawa Shrine, Shinagawa Ward.
